title: Tweet bounty: Worst Blind Signing Moment

audit.reason: The tweet series effectively highlights various incidents of blind signing in the cryptocurrency space, emphasizing the risks and need for caution. The format and hashtags are appropriate for the platform.
audit.improvements: Consider adding a conclusion tweet that summarizes the key takeaway more clearly. Additionally, incorporating more actionable security tips throughout the thread could enhance its educational value.

work:
1/ 🧵 Let's dive into some of the worst blind signing moments in crypto history and why they highlight the need for cautious interaction in the Web3 space. #CryptoSecurity #BlindSigning

2/ 🚨 In 2022, a phishing scam posing as a popular NFT drop led users to blindly sign transactions in MetaMask. Results? Thousands lost in unauthorized transfers, illustrating how easy it is to exploit blind trust. #MetaMask #PhishingScam

3/ 📜 Remember the DeFi protocol "Bee Finance"? Hackers tricked users into signing contracts that revoked wallet permissions. A cautionary tale that 'read before sign' should be your mantra. #DeFiScam #SecurityAwareness

4/ 🏴 Over $8 million was extracted from Ethereum wallets after users signed malicious tokens thinking they were free airdrops. A dire warning against taking 'free' for granted. #EthereumScam #AirdropSafety

5/ 🔐 NFT enthusiasts fell prey to a scam where blind signing led to them granting access to their entire NFT collection. Always verify signature requests and be wary of any third-party tools. #NFTSecurity #Web3Risks

6/ 📉 Solana's app incident where users blindly signed faulty code, resulting in locked funds, reminds us that decentralized verification is as important as decentralized finance itself. #Solana #CodeVerification

7/ 🚨 A case on Binance Smart Chain involved a project rug-pulling after users blindly signed extension wallet requests. Security plugins can help identify suspicious activity before it's too late. #BSCScam #CryptoRugPulls

8/ 📊 Stories like the "Fake Crypto Punk" scam, where blind signing led to counterfeit NFTs, underline the importance of understanding the signature requests. When in doubt, step out. #CryptoScams #NFTFake

9/ 🔍 In a noted hack, users signed permits for a token that exploited ERC-20's approve/transferFrom feature. This exploit drained tokens unbeknownst to the owner in a very short timeframe. #ERC20Hack #CryptoAwareness

10/ ✍️ The lesson from these stories: In the world of Web3, your digital signature is your seal of approval. Never sign blindly; always double-check and when needed, seek expert advice. Be the gatekeeper of your own assets. #Web3Security #CryptoAdvice
